summary: Implement a comprehensive Vitest-based unit testing suite for src/lib/concerts.ts (2076 lines, 25+ exported functions) to achieve 80% code coverage. The testing architecture follows the project's existing patterns: co-located __tests__/ directories, GDPR-compliant test fixtures (anonymized PII), mocked Prisma client for database operations, and isolated unit tests for pure functions. The test suite covers CRUD operations, cursor-based pagination (bidirectional), filtering (user/band/city/year), statistics caching via unstable_cache, spending aggregation, and edge cases (duplicate detection, missing data, null handling). Vitest is configured with TypeScript support, reactive mocking, and the @vitest/ui for watch mode. No breaking changes to existing code; testing adds only devDependencies and test files.

changes.description: Vitest configuration file. Sets up TypeScript support, Prisma mocking, coverage thresholds (80% target), UI reporter, and environment variables for tests.
Global test setup file. Mocks Next.js components (Image, Link), initializes Prisma mock factory, sets up environment variables for tests (no real credentials), and configures spy/mock utilities.

Comprehensive unit test suite for src/lib/concerts.ts covering 25+ exported functions. Organized by functional area: utilities (getStartOfToday, parseSupportingActIds), CRUD (create/update/delete), queries (getBy*, getAll*), pagination (getConcertsPaginated, bidirectional), filtering, statistics, and edge cases. Uses vitest test() and describe() blocks with consistent naming pattern test_{function}_{condition}_{expected}. Prisma client mocked via vi.mock(), test data uses anonymized fixtures.

changes.description: Comprehensive testing guide for the project. Covers: Vitest patterns, Prisma mocking, external API mocking (Last.fm, Photon), privacy flag testing (hideLocationPublic, hideCostPublic, isPublic), GDPR compliance requirements (no real PII in tests, anonymized fixtures, test data cleanup), running tests in watch mode, coverage reporting. Mandatory reference for all contributors.

Add testing dependencies and scripts. Install vitest@latest, @vitest/ui, @vitest/coverage-v8, vi-mock-deep. Add scripts: 'test' (run vitest), 'test:watch' (watch mode), 'test:coverage' (coverage report).

functions_or_methods.name: scripts
functions_or_methods.description: Add test, test:watch, test:coverage scripts using vitest

changes.description: Add reference to TESTING.md in the Testing Commands section and Code Patterns. Link to comprehensive testing guidelines for contributors.
TypeScript configuration for test files. Extends base tsconfig.json with test-specific paths and compilerOptions.

context7_lookups_needed.query: test configuration with TypeScript, Prisma mocking, coverage setup, UI reporter
watch mode UI and interactive test results
deeply mocking Prisma client with typed methods

data_flow: Test → vi.mock(Prisma) → Execute function with mocked DB → Assert output → Reset mocks

risks.description: Prisma mocking complexity - deeply nested relations (concert.bands[].band) are difficult to mock correctly
risks.mitigation: Use vi-mock-deep for typed deep mocking; create helper prisma-mock.ts with factory functions; test with realistic mock data

risks.description: Test data contains PII (User emails, locations, costs) - GDPR compliance risk
risks.mitigation: Use test-fixtures.ts with anonymized data only (test-user-@test.example.com, mock UUIDs); enforce via TESTING.md guidelines and code review; never commit real user data

risks.description: unstable_cache() behavior in tests - may not invalidate correctly in test environment
risks.mitigation: Mock next/cache unstable_cache directly; verify cache tag revalidation logic separately; document in TESTING.md

risks.description: Cover all 25+ functions with edge cases - high test maintenance burden
risks.mitigation: Phased approach: MVP covers core CRUD and pagination (12 tests = 80% coverage); remaining functions deferred. Use test naming convention for easy discovery. Document which functions have deferred tests in TESTING.md.

risks.description: Test runner performance with large Prisma schema mocking
risks.mitigation: Run tests serially for initial stability; use coverage.autoUpdate to avoid manual threshold adjustments; consider parallelization in future

open_questions: Should tests use an in-memory SQLite database (via @prisma/internals) or continue with vi.mock approach? Mock is simpler but less realistic.
How deep should privacy flag testing go (hideLocationPublic, hideCostPublic, isPublic)? Should this be separate integration tests or part of concerts.test.ts?
Should unstable_cache() behavior be tested (requires mocking next/cache) or documented as external dependency?
Acceptance threshold: 80% coverage may not catch complex branching in transformConcertSync/transformConcertsBatch. Should we target 85% instead?
